customer_anxiety_profile: Low warmth / transactional engagement. Beth is cooperative and confirmed the appointment without hesitation, but her responses throughout were minimal. No expressed enthusiasm for solar — primary motivation is bill reduction ($500–$1,000/month with ComEd). Physical limitation (wheelchair) affects her ability to retrieve and send documents; the closer should not assume she can navigate digital tools quickly.
unresolved_concerns: Utility bill not captured — Beth said she would call back with it; Carter routed follow-up to a personal direct number with no structured deadline or fallback.
Credit score not confirmed above threshold — Beth said she has 'a bigger credit score' but gave no number. Carter accepted this as sufficient. Closer should verify.
If this is a paid lead, the bill is a hard requirement before the sit qualifies — closer should confirm bill status before the Monday 3PM appointment.
sensitive_topics_raised: Beth disclosed she is in a wheelchair, which affects her ability to retrieve documents and navigate technology. The closer should not ask Beth to pull up materials quickly or assume she can multitask during the call.
exact_promises_rep_made: Carter gave Beth a personal direct number to text the utility bill or call for help — 'That's my direct number. So you could text it there. If you have issues or just wanna read it off, just call that number.'
closer_should_know: The utility bill is not in hand. Beth is in a wheelchair and said she would need to call back with it — Carter routed this to a personal number with no deadline. Before the Monday 3PM sit, someone needs to confirm the bill was received or arrange a verbal read-back at the start of the appointment. Do not assume the bill will arrive on its own.
